sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: Grimmsnarl is the NIGHTMARE of the  Fantasy Cup... | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Grimmsnarl lead boosts with Power-Up Punch to pressure shields, Azumarill is a bulky neutral safe swap, and Aegislash closes out games once shields are down.
performanceNotes: Went 5-0 in the featured set and continued winning beyond it
Grimmsnarl's boosted Sucker Punch fast-move pressure is the team's selling point
Shadow Waterfall Empoleon identified as a core breaker for this team

sources.author: YasserPoGO
sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Fantasy Cup | Great League | Pokemon GO PvP

description: PvPoke top-rated team. Creator presents it but explicitly says he doesn't believe it's the strongest and dislikes Aegislash in this cup.
performanceNotes: PvPoke #1 rated team
Creator does not endorse Aegislash due to Stunfisk vulnerability

description: ABB double steel with Altaria lead. Stunfisk safe swaps to bait out Empoleon counters (fighters/fire), then Empoleon closes with two shields. Creator says he will personally run this team.

sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Fantasy Cup | Great League | Pokemon GO PvP
sources.author: YasserPoGO

performanceNotes: ABB bait-trap format — Stunfisk draws out Empoleon's counters
Two-shield Empoleon in the back as win condition
Creator's personal pick
5-0 first set, 10-2 overall
Waterfall Empoleon hits Steel types and Aegislash extremely hard compared to standard Empoleon
Azumarill on Hydro Pump instead of Ice Beam provides extra closing power

sources.videoTitle: *INSTANT 5-0* to start the Fantasy Cup with Waterfall Shadow Empoleon! | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: HomeSliceHenry

description: Giratina Origin lead with Shadow Waterfall Empoleon safe swap and Azumarill closer. Built around the Giratina Origin + Shadow Empoleon core, with Waterfall providing incredible fast move pressure into Steel types and Azumarill cleaning up in zero-shield scenarios.

Shadow Excadrill lead team built around its faster pacing compared to Galarian Stunfisk, paired with bulky Shadow Altered Giratina and Azumarill to absorb hits and leverage shield advantage for the glassy Excadrill.

sources.author: MarckPoGoW
sources.videoTitle: IS EXCADRILL BETTER Than STUNFISK in the Fantasy Cup! Pokemon Go PvP   Trim

performanceNotes: Excadrill outpaces Galarian Stunfisk in the head-to-head lead, winning the mirror typing matchup
Team went undefeated across all shown battles with multiple opponent resignations
Azumarill's bulk allows it to tank multiple Earthquakes from Stunfisk and wall several meta threats
Performed well at 2800-2925 ELO approaching Legend rank
Giratina Origin's ghost typing and Shadow Claw pressure dominates Dublade and Aegislash matchups
Struggles against the Home Slice Henry variant running Shadow Empoleon in place of Galarian Stunfisk
description: Spicy shiny Giratina Origin lead paired with two OG picks in the back. Azumarill runs Hydro Pump specifically to handle steel types like Aegislash and Galarian Stunfisk common in the Fantasy Cup meta.

sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion
sources.videoTitle: I Finally Used my Great League Shiny Giratina!! | Pokémon GO Battle League

performanceNotes: Took two trainers to Rank 1 in the world on the GBL leaderboard
Galarian Stunfisk was the MVP — many opponents were core broken by its Ground+Rock coverage
Azumarill excels as a closer when shields are down due to its massive bulk

sources.videoTitle: I REACHED RANK ONE IN THE WORLD IN THE GO BATTLE LEAGUE! | Pokémon GO PvP
sources.author: HomeSliceHenry

description: ABA Steel team originally created by Shadowlord. Galarian Stunfisk leads with unwallable Ground+Rock coverage, Alolan Sandslash pivots as the Steel safe swap, and Azumarill closes running Ice Beam + Hydro Pump instead of the typical Play Rough to hit dragons faster with super-effective damage.

performanceNotes: Corviknight is a steel that doesn't lose to ground
Don't swap out of the lead — play it out
Turtonator Overheat does ~40% to Azumarill even in a bad matchup

sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Fantasy Cup | Great League | Pokemon GO PvP
sources.author: YasserPoGO

description: ABA steel team. Corviknight leads and stays in — never swap out. Play out soft wins/losses, then farm energy on Turtonator or Magnezone in the back.

performanceNotes: Doublade's Sacred Fire covers steel matchups better than Aegislash
Struggles into opposing Aegislash without Shadow Ball

sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Fantasy Cup | Great League | Pokemon GO PvP
sources.author: YasserPoGO

description: Creator's improved variant of PvPoke's top team, replacing Aegislash with Doublade for Sacred Fire pressure on steels. Doublade lacks Shadow Ball which is a noted weakness vs Aegislash.

performanceNotes: Psychic on Alolan Ninetales flips Poison matchups like Galarian Weezing
Chilling Water debuffs opponent attack and provides neutral Steel coverage
Sucker Punch Wormadam Trash generates strong farm-down pressure and energy advantages

sources.author: MarckPoGoW
sources.videoTitle: THE STRANGEST ALOLAN NINETALES MOVESET YOUVE EVER SEEN in the Fantasy Cup! Pokémon Go PvP

description: Shadow Dragonite lead with Wormadam Trash safe swap and Alolan Ninetales closer running the unusual Chilling Water + Psychic moveset. Psychic hits Poison types super effectively while Chilling Water provides neutral damage into Steels that normally wall Ninetales.

sources.videoTitle: This FORGOTTEN Flygon Moveset RIPS Through GBL [Pokémon GO PvP]
sources.author: ItsAXN

description: Shadow Flygon with the forgotten Sand Attack fast move leads this Fantasy Cup team, providing more consistent fast move pressure than Mud Shot at the cost of slightly slower energy generation. Empoleon and Togekiss round out the backline.
performanceNotes: Sand Attack's fast move pressure shreds opponents, especially after Sand Tomb defense debuffs
Scorching Sands provides knockout power while Sand Tomb baits shields effectively
Struggles in the Flygon mirror where Sand Attack gets outpaced by Mud Shot variants

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: Shadow Giratina is NASTY in the Fantasy Cup! #19 in the world! | Pokémon GO PvP

description: Shadow Giratina Altered lead with Shadow Waterfall Empoleon safe swap and Florges closer. Florges provides unique coverage in the Fantasy Cup meta, closing out games that Giratina and Empoleon set up.
performanceNotes: Reached Legend rank in only 380 total battles on wife's account
Ranked #19 in the world
Florges proved to be an exceptional closer with strong Fantasy Cup coverage
Reached 2700 ELO
Double weak to ground — Mud Slap users like Excadrill are the worst-case scenario
Shadow Weezing's Brutal Swing chosen over Overheat for ghost coverage and rapid 8-energy pacing

sources.videoTitle: Up to 2700 ELO with an ALL SAFE TEAM | Fantasy Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Triple shadow 'all safe' team built around three individually safe Pokémon. Shadow Weezing leads with Brutal Swing for ghost coverage and fast energy cycling, Shadow Giratina Altered is the safe swap that walls nearly everything without a dark-type answer, and Shadow Empoleon closes.

performanceNotes: Started 5-0, reached 2585 ELO
Triple ground coverage dominates the steel-heavy meta
Vulnerable to double mudslap users in the back line

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: TRIPLE GROUND BREAKS a STEEL DOMINATED META | Fantasy Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Triple ground team designed to break the steel-dominated Fantasy Cup meta. Shadow Flygon leads with Sand Tomb debuffs and Twister for dragon coverage, backed by double Steel/Ground in the back.

sources.videoTitle: REGISTEEL IS BACK!!! (Fantasy Cup)
sources.author: CalebPeng

description: ABA Steel strategy with Registeel lead, Waterfall Primarina safe swap, and Forretress closer. On bad leads, sacrifice Registeel to farm energy/chip damage, then clean up with Primarina and Forretress in the back.
performanceNotes: Went 5-0 in the first set and continued strong in the second set
ABA Steel structure provides resilience against bad leads — sacrifice lead Registeel and sweep with the backline
Creator calls the core 'very reliable' and says it 'breaks a lot of things in Fantasy Cup'
Went 11-4 and climbed 200 Elo with this team
Gabite beats Ferrothorn and Fortress in the 0-shield and pressures Galarian Stunfisk with Dig
Togetic's Heat Wave provides crucial fire coverage against steel types that wall the rest of the team

sources.videoTitle: TOGETIC has AMAZING COVERAGE for Fantasy Cup Meta | Fantasy Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Gabite lead with Jirachi and Togetic as interchangeable safe swaps. Gabite's Dragon/Ground coverage is only resisted by Corviknight and fairy flyers, while Jirachi's Steel typing and Togetic's Heat Wave cover steels, fairies, and Azumarill.

performanceNotes: Went positive overall despite Tinkaton struggling against prevalent Steels and Waters
Giratina and Turtonator carried the team and multiple other teams this season
Tinkaton's Gigaton Hammer did far less than expected when resisted due to low attack stat

sources.videoTitle: Beyond the HYPE: TINKATON'S LIMITATIONS on FULL DISPLAY | Fantasy Cup Team | GO Battle League
sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO

description: Revised Tinkaton team with Giratina as safe swap and Turtonator as closer. Went positive overall but largely carried by Giratina and Turtonator rather than Tinkaton itself.

performanceNotes: Climbed from 2520 to 2620 in just 2-3 sets after failing with Lucario, Dublade, and other teams
Creator considers Giratina Altered the best safe swap in the Fantasy Cup meta
Strong meta coverage with fire pressure from Turtonator and Weezing punishing common Steel/Fairy/Ice types

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: After LOSING ELO, I WENT BACK TO MY TOP ELO GAINING TEAM | Fantasy Cup Team | GO Battle League

description: Turtonator lead with Giratina Altered safe swap running Brutal Swing instead of Shadow Sneak, and Galarian Weezing closer. Creator's go-to Fantasy Cup team that he consistently returns to after losing ELO with other teams.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: 12-3 to SEASON HIGH ELO with SMART DOUBLE DRAGON TEAM | Fantasy Cup Team | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Smart double dragon team exploiting the steel/fairy-heavy Fantasy Cup meta. Turtonator's fire typing neutralizes dragon weaknesses to fairy and ice, Galarian Weezing handles Azumarill and fairies, and Giratina Altered roams freely as a closer since dark/ghost threats are suppressed by the meta.
performanceNotes: Went 12-3 to season high ELO
Giratina Altered is surprisingly safe as closer since Guzzlord and other dark/ghost threats are scared off by the fairy/steel meta
Shadow Galarian Weezing's Overheat provides surprise coverage against steel safe swaps
Reached rank 5 globally on the GBL leaderboard — creator's highest placement of the season
Kingambit's dark/steel typing core breaks many popular Fantasy Cup teams (Morgrem, Doublade, Aegislash, Guzzlord)
Team is soft to Azumarill but can manage it by tanking Play Rough with Giratina Origin and farming to 100 energy

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: I hit *RANK 5 IN THE WORLD* with a Pokémon almost NO ONE believed in... | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Kingambit lead as a dark/steel core breaker to many Fantasy Cup teams, with Togetic safe swap running Dazzling Gleam and Heatwave (which the creator considers strictly the correct moveset), and Giratina Origin as the closer.

sources.author: DanOttawaPOGO
sources.videoTitle: Beyond the HYPE: TINKATON'S LIMITATIONS on FULL DISPLAY | Fantasy Cup Team | GO Battle League

description: Initial Tinkaton team used for the first battle before the creator switched lineups. Tinkaton leads with Galarian Weezing and Turtonator as backline support.
performanceNotes: Switched away from this team after the first battle due to Tinkaton's poor performance against Steels
